titel: The Commission is urging the Netherlands to improve competition in the market for international passenger rail services.
html: European Commission Press Release, Brussels, June 29, 2026. The European Commission has informed the Netherlands that the rules for allocating capacity on the Dutch national railway network may be in violation of EU competition law.
tldr: The EU is criticizing the Netherlands for regulations governing train traffic on their railways, arguing that these rules may hinder competition. The goal is to make it easier for different companies to operate international passenger trains.
lattlast:
The European Commission, the executive body of the EU, has contacted the Netherlands regarding regulations for train traffic. They believe that the current rules governing the use of railway infrastructure may be in violation of EU regulations on free competition.

This concerns making it easier for different train companies to operate passenger services between countries. If the rules are too strict or favor certain companies, it becomes more difficult for other companies to compete for customers in the international market.

Therefore, the Netherlands needs to review its regulations to ensure that all train companies have equal opportunities to use the railway network. This is important for creating a better and more diverse rail service for people traveling between different countries in Europe.
